Since we have made it usefuWrite And Read – WordPress theme Free Download WordPress Themes Theme Preview This is a child theme of Blogrid. Version: 1.0 Theme Homepage Write and Read is a clean and creative blog theme for writers and bloggers. The theme is responsive, making it fully compatible with mobile phones, desktop computers, and tablets such as iPads. With a multi-purpose design that is minimal and simple, you can use it as a newspaper or magazine to post your news about your personal life, food, entertainment, gaming, or anything else. The readability is optimized through great typography. Page builders work fine with the theme, and since it's full width by default, you can create a landing page or one-page website easily to visualize your business, agency, or consultant company. Since we have made it useful for various purposes, it's also perfect for showcasing portfolios, blogs, or online magazines. Key Features: - Responsive Design - Minimal and Simple Design - Great Typography for Readability - Compatible with Page Builders - Full Width Layout for Flexibility - Multi-Purpose Use (blog, news, portfolio, etc.) Download the Write And Read WordPress theme

DatabaseReadReplicaRequired: Type: String Default: 'No' AllowedValues: - 'Yes' - 'No' Description: The database cluster needs a read replica .... Conditions: CreateReadReplica: It looks like you've defined the parameter and condition for creating a read replica, but it seems you didn't complete the `CreateReadReplica` condition in your CloudFormation template. Here's how you can define that condition based on the `DatabaseReadReplicaRequired` parameter: ```yaml Conditions: CreateReadReplica: !Equals [ !Ref DatabaseReadReplicaRequired, 'Yes' ] ``` With this condition defined, you can use it to control whether the read

texts.full_text: Dakota, Respondent and Appellee. No. 14968. Supreme Court of South Dakota. Considered on Briefs January 15, 1986. Decided December 23, 1986. *141 Cynthia A. howard, Northern Hills Public Defender's Office, Deadwood, for petitioner and appellant. Robert Mayer, Asst. Atty. Gen., Pierre, for respOndent and appellee; Mark V. Meierhenry, Atty. Gen., Pierre, on brief. MORGAN, Justice. This is an appeal from an order quashing a writ of habeas corpus originally issued on the petition of Gary Loop (Loop) which alleged, among other things, an unconstitutional deprivation of effective assistance of appellate counsel. We reverse and remand. On November 4, 1981, Loop was charged with the offense of sexual contact with a child under fifteen yFacts: Gary Lynn Loop ("Loop") appealed from an order quashing his writ of habeas corpus filed by him as petitioner-appellant against Herman Solem ("Solem"), Warden at South Dakota State Penitentiary, who was respondent-appellee. The appeal challenged the constitutionality of deprivation and denial of effective assistance of appellate counsel. Loop's conviction for sexual contact with a child under fifteen years old in November 1981 resulted from his trial on January 25-30, 1984 ( Loop v. State , SD., No. CR79-67). The Court of Appeals affirmed the conviction and sentence on May 16, 1985. Loop sought habeas corpus relief in June 1985 based primarily upon ineffective assistance claims against his appellate counsel from the Office of Attorney General (OAG), which was appointed to represent him during post-conviction proceedings. The Circuit Court denied Loop's petition and quashed the writ on November 26, 1985. Issue: WhetherLoop received effective assistance of appellate counsel under Strickland v.Washington , U.S., --- S.Ct. [---]
